preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an intent-aware, ai-first design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is an intent-aware, AI-first design system built for startups. Frames the problem space and signals relevance to the audience, anchoring the narrative before any persuasion begins.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: With context set, the Validator needs to understand what MODE actually delivers — adaptive intent detection, AI-driven component logic, and design-to-code acceleration. Presenting capabilities and outcome promises here builds a concrete mental model of the product before asking for trust.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require social proof before they internalize value claims. Placing credibility after value lets startup founders see who else has adopted MODE — customer logos, testimonials from comparable startups, and authority signals that confirm the product is real and trusted.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: The Validator archetype demands hard proof. Metrics (e.g., time-to-ship reduction, conversion lift, adoption stats) and case study data from startup customers substantiate the value claims and credibility signals, closing the skepticism gap that sits between interest and commitment.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: At consideration stage with a Validator, presenting plan comparison and pricing transparently signals confidence and respects the audience's need to evaluate. Positioned after evidence, it channels accumulated conviction into a concrete choice frame.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately follows Decision per structural rules. A clear CTA — start a paid plan or trial — captures the momentum built through value, proof, and pricing. Copy reinforces low-risk commitment aligned with startup budget sensitivity.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat provides secondary paths for Validators not yet ready: documentation links, a comparison guide, demo booking, and footer navigation. Ensures no visitor exits without an alternative route back into the funnel.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is an intent-aware, AI-first design system built for startups. Frames the problem space and signals relevance to the audience, anchoring the narrative before any persuasion begins.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require social proof before they internalize value claims. Placing credibility after value lets startup founders see who else has adopted MODE — customer logos, testimonials from comparable startups, and authority signals that confirm the product is real and trusted.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: The Validator archetype demands hard proof. Metrics (e.g., time-to-ship reduction, conversion lift, adoption stats) and case study data from startup customers substantiate the value claims and credibility signals, closing the skepticism gap that sits between interest and commitment.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Conversion stage pages benefit from minimal variant which strips nav_links and keeps only logo and single CTA — this reduces friction at the decision moment
Consideration/conversion funnel stage requires text-only variant: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic intelligence layer for code-first SaaS design systems. Components that resolve to the right behavior for every archetype, funnel stage, and context — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype benefits from outcome-oriented descriptions in the default 3-col-icon layout, which structures product capabilities for credibility and specificity

slots.headline: What MODE resolves — automatically.
slots.subhead: Eleven components. Four resolution dimensions. Zero manual variant decisions.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components evaluate archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode on every render — so the right experience ships by default, not by convention.

features.title: IA planner
features.description: Go from brief to full page architecture in seconds. Claude Opus maps the intent model to a component layout before you write a line of markup.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Evaluate three differentiated directions without three separate briefs.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. No manual accent assignment — the system knows which components carry the most weight per funnel stage.

features.title: Behavioral tokens
features.description: Copy density, evidence requirements, and CTA rules are archetype-driven. The system enforces the right register without a style guide review loop.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: Eleven components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. A shared contract between your design intelligence and your codebase.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype optimized pages benefit from structured argument depth; with-callout variant separates key proof points for visual emphasis

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below — establishing credibility through both attributed narrative and brand recognition

slots.headline: Built for teams evaluating what comes after component libraries.
slots.quote: We were maintaining three variant sets for every major landing section — one for paid traffic, one for organic, one for retargeting. MODE collapsed that into a single source of truth. The resolution engine handles the branching logic we were doing by hand.
slots.attribution: Head of Growth Engineering, early-access customer

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires with-photo variant which increases engagement and perceived authenticity, and should always include role and company for credibility

page.reasoning: Validator contexts require with-source attribution — unsourced stats reduce trust, making source-attributed metrics essential for this archetype

slots.headline: What teams measure after adopting MODE.

stats.label: Median time from install to first intent-resolved page
More page variants evaluated per brief with multi-variant generation
Runtime dependencies — MODE lives in your repo, fully owned

page.reasoning: Conversion stage page requires with-cta variant to recapture departing users at the moment of exit on key conversion pages
